title: Ancient Skull Reveals Human History

activity_question: What did Andrew Wyeth secretly create?
follow_up_question: Why do you think an artist might keep their work secret for a long time?

title: Tiny Davis: Jazz's Mighty Voice and Trumpet
story: Ernestine 'Tiny' Davis blasted onto the jazz scene with her powerful trumpet and voice. Born in Memphis on August 5, 1909, she defied her nickname with her huge musical talent. Davis became a star of the International Sweethearts of Rhythm, an all-female jazz band that wowed audiences during the 1940s. Her bold performances helped pave the way for women in jazz.
activity_question: What instrument did Tiny Davis play besides being a vocalist?
bonus_fact: The International Sweethearts of Rhythm was one of the first integrated all-women's bands in the United States.

title: Dancing Dreams Take Flight
story: Betty Oliphant pirouetted into the world on August 5, 1918, in London, England. This British-Canadian ballerina would go on to make a grand jeté in dance education. Oliphant's passion for ballet led her to co-found the National Ballet School of Canada, nurturing generations of talented dancers. Her legacy continues to inspire young performers to reach for the stars and dance their dreams.
activity_question: What did Betty Oliphant co-found?
activity_choices: The National Ballet School of Canada
activity_answer: The National Ballet School of Canada

title: A Missionary's Remarkable Journey Begins
story: Baby cries echoed in an English church as John Eliot was baptized in 1604. Little did anyone know this newborn would grow up to become a famous missionary. title: The Rubber Revolution Begins
